name: Double door grand entrance
description: A wide, ceremonial entrance with two doors placed adjacent in a shared frame. Symmetric hinges (left door hinge=left, right door hinge=right) let the pair swing open as a grand opening. A 4-wide accent lintel spans both doors, a row of stairs above overhangs outward for shelter, optional accent capstones decorate the lintel ends, and two lanterns flank the entry at head height for visibility on arrival.

required_furniture.rule: one accent column on each side of the door pair, 2 tall
4-wide accent course directly above the door pair, jamb-to-jamb
stairs row above the lintel, overhanging outward (facing opposite the door)
optional accent caps at the two lintel ends, toggled via `with_capstones`
one lantern outboard of each jamb at head height; toggled via `with_lanterns`

placement_rules: Two doors centered side by side
Symmetric hinges for grand entrance look
Capstones cap the lintel ends when height allows
Stair overhang faces opposite the door so the slope sheds rain outward
Wall axis is inferred from `facing` (north/south → wall runs along X; east/west → wall runs along Z)
lighting_ventilation: The double entry is the primary daylight aperture and the public face of the building; two flanking lanterns mark the threshold at night and pair with the lintel to signal a formal arrival point. Reserve at least one wall block between this skill's frame and any other window/door to keep the symmetric reading intact.

medieval.notes: Stone-brick frame with oak doors — reads as a keep gate or longhouse main hall entry.

modern.notes: Flush smooth-stone surround with paired iron doors; lanterns optional and often replaced by redstone lamps elsewhere.

fantasy.notes: Purpur frame and dark oak twin doors evoke a temple or guildhall portal; flanking sea lanterns recommended.

python_skill_signature: build(aabb: AABB, materials: Materials, style: str, *, facing: str = 'south', with_lanterns: bool = True, with_capstones: bool = True) -> list[Op]
